description: Wire all 8 unwired admin panel pages to their existing backend API routes. Replace hardcoded mock arrays with fetch() calls that include loading, error, and data states. Keep mock data as fallback when API calls fail.
steps: Wire apps/admin/src/app/page.tsx (Dashboard): Add useEffect that fetches /api/stats and /api/health. Replace systemStats array with data from /api/stats (users.total, users.active etc). Replace appHealthData with apps array from /api/health. Show LoadingState while fetching, ErrorState on failure, fall back to existing mock data on error.

Wire apps/admin/src/app/apps/page.tsx: Add useEffect that fetches /api/health. Replace appsData array with the apps array returned from health endpoint (map name, port, status fields). Add loading/error states.

Wire apps/admin/src/app/users/page.tsx: Add useEffect that fetches /api/users?page=1&pageSize=20. Replace usersData array with data from API response. The API already returns {success, data: users[], metadata: {total, page, pageSize}}. Add pagination support using metadata.totalPages. Add loading/error states.

Wire apps/admin/src/app/services/page.tsx: Add useEffect that fetches /api/health to get the services list, then for each service fetch /api/services/{id}/logs to get logs. Replace servicesData array. Add loading/error states.

Wire apps/admin/src/app/ai/page.tsx: Add useEffect that fetches /api/stats (or create /api/ai/stats route if needed). Replace providers and modelUsage arrays. The AI page needs provider data - create a new API route at apps/admin/src/app/api/ai/stats/route.ts that returns AI provider mock data (since no actual AI metrics DB exists yet). The page should fetch from /api/ai/stats.

Wire apps/admin/src/app/security/page.tsx: Add useEffect that fetches /api/audit?action=LOGIN&action=LOGIN_FAILED (reuse audit endpoint). Replace authEvents and alerts arrays. Add loading/error states.

Wire apps/admin/src/app/database/page.tsx: Create new API route at apps/admin/src/app/api/database/stats/route.ts that returns pool info and table stats (can use prisma.$queryRaw for pg_stat_user_tables or return reasonable defaults). Wire the page to fetch from /api/database/stats. Replace tables and slowQueries arrays.

Wire apps/admin/src/app/settings/page.tsx: Create new API route at apps/admin/src/app/api/settings/route.ts with GET (read settings) and PUT (save settings). Wire the page to fetch/save settings. Replace the hardcoded initialFlags with data from the new API. Add loading/error states.
acceptance_criteria: All 8 admin pages have useEffect with fetch() calls to their respective API endpoints
Each page shows a loading state (LoadingState component or Skeleton) while data loads
Each page shows an error state (ErrorState component) if the fetch fails
Each page falls back to existing mock data if the fetch fails (graceful degradation)
Two new API routes created: /api/database/stats and /api/settings (GET + PUT)
